ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Noodle. FOODON_00002473 (noodle soup) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (noodle soup) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Thai tom yum beef noodle soup in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific Thai noodle soup dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Noodle weighs approximately 500 g, comprising noodles, beef brisket, beef tendon, vegetables, and tom yum broth.

nutrient_source.title: Beef noodle soup, Thai-style — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables for Southeast Asian noodle soups
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central / FAO INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Noodle exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for beef brisket (USDA FDC ID 174032), beef tendon (analogous collagen-rich cut), rice noodles (USDA FDC ID 168917), and tom yum broth components (lemongrass, galangal, fish sauce, chilli, lime juice). FAO/INFOODS SEA composition data for Thai noodle soups was used to cross-validate the composite estimate. Serving size set at 500 g (typical restaurant bowl). Per-100 g values derived from the weighted composite.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice noodles and mixed noodle soup analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Noodle. GI estimated at 45 based on the closest analogous food: rice noodle soup (GI ~40–50, low category) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. The dominant glycemic carbohydrate source is rice noodles; the protein and fat content from beef brisket and tendon further attenuate the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 42.5) / 100 = 19.1 (500 g serving × 8.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 42.5 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and tom yum broth
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this dish due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 420 mg per 100 g (2100 mg per standard 500 g bowl), which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg in a single serving; requesting reduced fish sauce or consuming a smaller portion is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from rice noodles, partially offset by protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the rice noodle base contributes a moderate glycemic load of approximately 19 per bowl; choosing a smaller portion, requesting extra vegetables, or substituting with glass noodles (lower GI) can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef brisket and tendon, combined with high sodium broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate intake of this dish due to its saturated fat content from beef brisket and tendon (estimated 1.0 g per 100 g) and its high sodium load from the tom yum broth; opting for a smaller serving and requesting less broth can reduce both saturated fat and sodium exposure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Noodle is a Thai noodle soup dish featuring rice noodles served in a hot, sour, and spicy tom yum broth, loaded with slow-braised beef brisket and gelatinous beef tendon. The broth is prepared by simmering lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, fresh chilli, fish sauce, and lime juice, producing the characteristic tom yum flavour profile of sharp acidity, herbal fragrance, and moderate heat. Beef brisket is braised until tender and yielding, while beef tendon is cooked until soft and collagen-rich, providing a distinctive gelatinous mouthfeel. The dish is typically garnished with bean sprouts, fresh herbs, and sliced chilli. In Singapore, this dish is served at Thai restaurants and food delivery platforms as a hearty main course. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein from beef (approximately 6.2 g per 100 g), moderate carbohydrate from rice noodles (approximately 8.5 g per 100 g), and a low-to-moderate glycemic index of approximately 45. The primary nutritional concern is its high sodium content from fish sauce and broth, estimated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g or 2100 mg per standard 500 g bowl, making it a high-sodium dish relevant to hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The collagen content from beef tendon may contribute to joint health. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans and contains fish allergens from fish sauce.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Noodle was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables as a named dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for beef brisket, rice noodles, and tom yum broth ingredients, cross-validated against FAO/INFOODS SEA noodle soup data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (rice noodle soup) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA data; no single approved-source entry for this exact dish was identified. Serving size of 500 g is based on typical Thai restaurant bowl portions in Singapore. Sentinel value -1 is used for the following micronutrients because they are not reliably reported in the component sources (USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA) used for this composite estimate and cannot be defensibly assigned without a direct assay: vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data; selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g as beef brisket and tendon contain negligible industrial trans fats; any naturally occurring trans fats (conjugated linoleic acid) are below the reporting threshold and confirmed as trace. Halal status is marked false as fish sauce sourcing and preparation method at the specific restaurant (Saap Saap Thai - Funan) cannot be confirmed from available data; consumers requiring halal certification should verify directly with the restaurant. The dish is served at Saap Saap Thai - Funan on field survey data at SGD 12.90.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed canonical English food name from input menu name, removing promotional prefix 'T6'.
Added Thai native-script local name as required by G6 for Thai cuisine.
Built composite nutrient estimate from USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA sources as no exact dish entry was found in primary regional sources.
Estimated GI from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (rice noodle soup) with full documentation.
Selected FoodOn and FoodEx2 closest_parent codes for noodle soup with gap notes; set SNOMED CT to none.

Revised 'notes' field in iteration 3 to explicitly document every -1 sentinel value with individual field-level justification statements, resolving g3_numeric_hygiene failure from iteration 2 validator feedback.
